perturbation.original_text: 2.2.2 Process for Exercising Right of First Offer . Evolent shall notify ABCO in writing in the event Evolent desires to receive any of the above services (“ First Offer Services ”). ABCO shall have a period of thirty (30) days (“ First Offer Services Evaluation Period ”) within which to conduct its evaluation and diligence with respect to providing such First Offer Services. Evolent shall cooperate with ABCO and provide reasonable assistance and information to ABCO to enable ABCO to conduct its evaluation. Within five (5) business days after the expiration of such First Offer Services Evaluation Period, ABCO shall notify Evolent in writing whether or not it wishes to provide such services to Evolent. If ABCO elects to provide such First Offer Services, Evolent agrees to engage in good faith negotiations with ABCO, on an exclusive basis for a period of sixty (60) days, regarding the specific terms covering such First Offer Services.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.2.2 Process for Exercising Right of First Offer . Evolent shall notify ABCO in writing in the event Evolent desires to receive any of the above services (“ First Offer Services ”). ABCO shall have a reasonable period of time to conduct its evaluation and diligence with respect to providing such First Offer Services. Evolent shall cooperate with ABCO and provide assistance and information to ABCO to enable ABCO to conduct its evaluation. ABCO shall notify Evolent in writing whether or not it wishes to provide such services to Evolent. If ABCO elects to provide such First Offer Services, Evolent agrees to engage in good faith negotiations with ABCO regarding the specific terms covering such First Offer Services.
perturbation.explanation: By removing specific timeframes (30 days for evaluation, 5 business days for notification, 60 days for exclusive negotiation), the "Right of First Offer" process becomes ambiguous. This contradicts the intent of Section 2.2, which outlines a structured process, leading to uncertainty in enforcement of ABCO's right to provide the services first. The lack of defined timeframes can also impact timely project planning.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 Right of First Offer and Process for Invoking : 2.2.1 Applicable Services . Throughout the Term, ABCO shall have the right of first offer to provide the following services to Evolent and Evolent’s customers: (a) Physician practice management consulting and other physician practice management services; (b) Physician incentive design services; (c) Medical home development consulting services; and (d) Performance analytics shared with physicians with the intent to improve outcomes. 2.2.2 Process for Exercising Right of First Offer . Evolent shall notify ABCO in writing in the event Evolent desires to receive any of the above services (“ First Offer Services ”). ABCO shall have a period of thirty (30) days (“ First Offer Services Evaluation Period ”) within which to conduct its evaluation and diligence with respect to providing such First Offer Services. Evolent shall cooperate with ABCO and provide reasonable assistance and information to ABCO to enable ABCO to conduct its evaluation. Within five (5) business days after the expiration of such First Offer Services Evaluation Period, ABCO shall notify Evolent in writing whether or not it wishes to provide such services to Evolent. If ABCO elects to provide such First Offer Services, Evolent agrees to engage in good faith negotiations with ABCO, on an exclusive basis for a period of sixty (60) days, regarding the specific terms covering such First Offer Services.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific timeframes for ABCO to evaluate, notify, and negotiate, making the 'Right of First Offer' process ambiguous. This contradicts the contradicted text, which includes the original, explicit timeframes (30 days for evaluation, 5 business days for notification, and 60 days for negotiation), creating an inconsistency in the obligations and rights of each party, following the 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: 3.1 Fees; Timing of Payments : Evolent shall pay ABCO the applicable fees for the Services performed by ABCO pursuant to a Statement of Work, as follows: (a) unless the Parties otherwise agree in writing, a deposit of 50% of the total fees for the particular project shall be paid upon signing of the Statement of Work; (b) ABCO will submit an invoice to Evolent for the balance upon completion of the work; and (c) Evolent shall pay ABCO for such invoiced amount within thirty (30) days after receipt of such invoice.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.1 Fees; Timing of Payments : Evolent shall pay ABCO the applicable fees for the Services performed by ABCO pursuant to a Statement of Work, as follows: (a) a deposit for the project shall be paid upon signing of the Statement of Work; (b) ABCO will submit an invoice to Evolent for the balance upon completion of the work; and (c) Evolent shall pay ABCO for such invoiced amount after receipt of such invoice.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the '50%' and 'within thirty (30) days' introduces ambiguity in payment terms. This contradicts the original intent of Section 3.1 which specifies the amount of deposit and timeframe for balance payment, leading to potential disputes over when and how much Evolent owes ABCO. This contradicts the payment structure outlined in the original section, causing enforcement uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.1 Fees; Timing of Payments : Evolent shall pay ABCO the applicable fees for the Services performed by ABCO pursuant to a Statement of Work, as follows: (a) a deposit for the project shall be paid upon signing of the Statement of Work; (b) ABCO will submit an invoice to Evolent for the balance upon completion of the work; and (c) Evolent shall pay ABCO for such invoiced amount after receipt of such invoice.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes '50%' and 'within thirty (30) days' from the payment terms in Section 3.1. This creates ambiguity regarding the deposit amount and payment timeframe. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text in this case, no longer provides specific payment terms, directly conflicting with the original intent of Section 3.1, which explicitly stated the deposit percentage and payment deadline. This alteration, based on the Perturbation Explanation provided, introduces uncertainty, making the contract unenforceable regarding the specific amount and timing of payments. This falls under the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 5.1.1 During the Term, ABCO will pay Evolent a sales referral fee for any sale made by ABCO that resulted from a “net” new sale from a new customer that was not already in ABCO’s sales pipeline (i.e., a “warm lead”) or a then-current customer of ABCO generated directly by Evolent’s efforts and without any assistance from any ABCO employee or representative (including any Evolent employee that serves on the Board of Directors of ABCO), equal to 5% of the total ABCO revenue from the new customer during the initial twelve (12) months of services under the services agreement with such new customer.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.1.1 During the Term, ABCO will pay Evolent a sales referral fee for any sale made by ABCO generated directly by Evolent’s efforts, equal to a percentage of the total ABCO revenue from the new customer.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity by removing the criteria for a 'net new sale' and making the referral fee a 'percentage' without specifying the exact percentage. This contradicts the original clause which defines the specific conditions and percentage for the referral fee, creating uncertainty in its calculation and applicability and could create in-text contradiction throughout the document as it reffers the calculation of this percentage
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1.1 During the Term, ABCO will pay Evolent a sales referral fee for any sale made by ABCO generated directly by Evolent’s efforts, equal to a percentage of the total ABCO revenue from the new customer.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the specific criteria for a 'net new sale' and the fixed 5% referral fee, replacing it with a general 'percentage'. The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, now lacks the original specificity, creating ambiguity and contradiction. The original text defines the conditions and exact percentage, whereas the change text just makes it a percentage creating ambiguity and therefore contradiction
